title: BEAM Therapeutics Q3 2025 Financial Results and Clinical Updates
text: Beam Therapeutics reported a net loss of $112.7 million in Q3 2025, compared to a net loss of $96.7 million in Q3 2024. Total revenue was $9.7 million from license and collaboration agreements, a decrease from $14.3 million in the previous year. Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were $1.1 billion as of September 30, 2025, compared to $850.7 million as of December 31, 2024. Beam expects that its cash will enable the company to fund operations into 2028. The alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency program remains a top priority, with continued enrollment and dosing progress in the BEAM-302 Phase 1/2 trial. Updated data from the BEACON Phase 1/2 clinical trial of BEAM-101 in patients with sickle cell disease were accepted for presentation at the American Society of Hematology Annual Meeting. The first subject was dosed in a Phase 1 healthy volunteer clinical trial of BEAM-103, an anti-CD117 monoclonal antibody developed as part of the ESCAPE platform. Bristol Myers Squibb announced a definitive agreement to acquire Beam collaborator Orbital Therapeutics for $1.5 billion, with Beam holding 75 million shares of Orbital common stock, representing approximately 17% ownership.
